title: baptisia lactea
intro: Baptisia lactea, also known as white wild indigo, is a perennial plant species belonging to the pea family. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Baptisia lactea include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory infections. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to promote overall health and well-being.
